scope.purpose: Define the required binding chain for approval lineage, ensuring a merge proof can reconstruct the complete review → approval → validation → merge → proof chain with hash-verified continuity.

core_invariant: reviewed_object == merged_object — no mutation may occur between approval and merge

lineage_chain.binding_semantics: The review object must bind the exact head_sha of the PR at the time the review was submitted. A review submitted against a prior head_sha is stale and cannot contribute to merge legitimacy.

The PREO_CANDIDATE must be generated against the exact PR head_sha at merge time. Any push after PREO generation invalidates the PREO unless checks re-run against the new head_sha. The preo_id must be unique per PR + head_sha combination.

The SCO_CANDIDATE must be generated against the exact PR head_sha. For PRs touching governed paths (governance/**, .github/workflows/**, src/**, schema.sql, migrations/**, wrangler.toml), SCO is required. SCO absence for governed-path mutations produces NULL merge legitimacy.

Topology reconciliation must emit SAFE_TO_MERGE signal. This signal is evidence only — it may block a merge but cannot authorize one. direct_merge_authority must always be false. TOPOLOGY_DRIFT, GOVERNANCE_DIVERGENCE, or UNDECLARED_EXECUTION_SURFACE signals produce MERGE_LEGITIMACY_NULL.

The merge event must record both the reviewed head_sha and the resulting merge_commit_sha. For squash and rebase merges, merge_commit_sha will differ from head_sha — both must be captured. The merge_commit_sha is the canonical identifier of what actually entered main.
lineage_chain.exact_object_check: merge_commit_sha must be verified to derive from head_sha with no intervening unauthorized mutation

lineage_chain.binding_semantics: The merge proof must bind all prior lineage objects by their identifiers and hashes. The proof is the terminal record of the full review → approval → validation → merge chain. Each proof is single-use — reuse of a merge proof against a different merge event produces NULL.
lineage_chain.missing_proof_result: NULL — merge without proof is not a governed merge

lineage_reconstruction_requirements.description: A merge proof must allow full reconstruction of the approval lineage chain from position 1 through 6.
required_properties: Each stage object must be independently hash-verifiable
Each stage binding must reference the exact head_sha or merge_commit_sha as appropriate
No implicit or inferred lineage bindings are permitted
Missing any stage binding produces NULL merge legitimacy for the chain
Lineage chain must be deterministically canonicalized (deterministic JSON canonicalization)

exact_object_admission.enforcement_mechanism: PREO head_sha binding — the PREO is generated against the exact head_sha; if a push occurs after PREO generation, the PREO is invalidated unless re-run against the new head_sha
exact_object_admission.squash_rebase_handling: For squash and rebase merges, the proof must bind both head_sha (the reviewed object) and merge_commit_sha (the merged object). The merge_commit_sha must be derivable from head_sha without intervening unauthorized mutation.
exact_object_admission.merge_queue_gap: Merge queue may produce a combined commit SHA not directly derivable from the reviewed PR head_sha — this represents an exact-object gap requiring merge-queue-specific PREO regeneration to close

replay_controls.stale_approval_prevention: PREO head_sha binding ensures the approval is against the current head_sha — any push after approval requires re-run of required checks
replay_controls.approval_reuse_prevention: Each PREO binds a unique pr_number + head_sha combination; reuse produces PREO_INVALID
replay_controls.merge_lineage_uniqueness: Each merge proof binds a unique merge_commit_sha and invocation_nonce; replay of a prior proof against a new merge event produces NULL
replay_controls.replay_safe_admission: A merge is replay-safe only if: (1) PREO nonce is single-use, (2) merge_commit_sha is unique per merge event, (3) proof invocation_nonce is fresh
